name: Eggplant with Ham Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the butter and add the onions and garlic. Cook, stirring until onions are wilted about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms, lemon juice, salt, pepper, parsley, thyme, and basil. Cook over relatively high heat, stirring frequently until liquid has almost completely evaporated, 8 to 10 minutes.
2. Add ham and cook stirring for about 4 minutes. Add bread crumbs and Parmesan cheese.
3. Trim the ends and cut each eggplant into 1/2 inch slices (about 16 slices).
4. Pour flour into a baking dish and add salt and pepper to taste. Dredge eggplant slices in the flour mixture on both sides, shaking off excess.
5. Heat about 1/4 cup oil in a large heavy skillet and add as many eggplant slices as the skillet will hold. Cook until golden brown on one side (1 1/2 to 2 minutes), adding more oil little by little. Turn slices, cook until golden on that side, and drain on paper towels. Continue adding eggplant slices and oil until all have been cooked on both sides (total cooking time about 15 minutes).
6. Select a rectangular, square, or oval baking dish. Arrange smaller slices of eggplant on the bottom touching. Spoon filling in the center, spreading but not quite to edges, mounding slightly.
7. Sprinkle remaining bread crumbs and cheese over all.
8. Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
9. Place dish in oven and bake for 35 to 40 minutes. Remove dish and pour off accumulated fat. Let cool slightly.
10. Serve with tomato sauce.
Tomato Sauce:
2 Tablespoons Butter
1 1/2 Cups Finely Chopped Onions
1/2 Clove Garlic, finely minced
2 Cups Canned Tomatoes (blended or sieved)
1/4 Bay Leaf
Salt to taste
Pepper to taste
